briefing.opening: Good morning. Welcome to the climate briefing for April 3, 2026. Today, we will highlight the clear signs of climate change both domestically and internationally, along with the multifaceted efforts being made towards carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition.

sections.title: 🔴⚠️ Signs of Extreme Weather and New Threats
sections.content: Signs of extreme weather are becoming increasingly evident, with March recently being warmer than usual and spring becoming drier. This March was recorded as the ninth consecutive March with rising temperatures [7, 156]. Such temperature changes accelerate the migration of species, with reports indicating that species move 80km for every 1-degree Celsius rise in temperature [9]. In particular, 157 contaminated sites in the U.S. are vulnerable to climate change and face the risk of toxic substance leakage [5]. Furthermore, new challenges for energy transition are emerging, such as Google opting for gas-fired power generation instead of its carbon neutrality goals due to a recent surge in AI power demand [52, 162].

sections.title: 🇰🇷⚠️ Domestic Climate Adaptation Efforts and Changes in Agriculture
sections.content: Domestically, significant changes are observed in the agricultural sector due to climate change. Apple cultivation areas are seen shifting northward, and the National Science Museum has even held special lectures on this phenomenon [2, 9]. Local governments, including Pocheon and Anseong cities, are urging farmers to disinfect rice seeds, transplant at the appropriate time, and refrain from excessively early transplanting, emphasizing strengthened farm management in response to climate change [1, 6]. In Seocheon County, practical efforts to adapt to the changing climate environment continue, such as conducting education for high-quality rice production [8, 10].

sections.title: 🌱 Expansion of Carbon Neutrality Practices in Korea
sections.content: Various initiatives for achieving carbon neutrality are actively underway across the country. To commemorate Arbor Day, numerous organizations and local governments, including the Ministry of Patriots and Veterans Affairs, the Northern Regional Forest Service, and the Small Enterprise and Market Agency, hosted large-scale tree planting events, leading the way in practicing carbon neutrality [18, 20, 21, 37, 43, 44, 46, 56, 59, 65, 66, 68, 70, 71, 78, 82, 85, 87, 90, 91, 92, 94, 96, 100]. Additionally, citizen participation is being incentivized by recruiting participants for the vehicle carbon neutrality points system, offering benefits of up to 100,000 KRW for reducing mileage [27, 41, 47, 50, 54, 61, 62, 69, 75, 76, 79, 80, 81, 102, 105]. Shinhan Asset Management's 'Global Carbon Neutrality Solution Fund' has exceeded 100 billion KRW in assets under management and recorded a 105% return rate, demonstrating a positive outlook for the carbon neutrality investment market [23, 24, 25, 35, 60, 64, 72, 74, 84, 89, 95, 101]. Scientific and technological research for carbon neutrality is also actively progressing, with a Korea University research team identifying the core technological mechanism for converting carbon dioxide into resources [36, 40, 83].

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Renewable Energy Transition and Expanding Investment
sections.content: The global transition to renewable energy is accelerating, significantly impacting domestic and international economic and policy trends. Globally, 692 GW of new renewable energy capacity was added, accounting for 85% of all new power generation [107, 110]. France is launching a 12 GW renewable energy tender, demonstrating its resolve to expand renewable energy amidst the energy crisis [120]. Domestically, stock prices of new and renewable energy companies, such as HD Hyundai Energy Solutions, are surging, and investment expectations are rising [106, 108, 115, 125, 129, 139]. Successful energy transition models are being established, such as the Imha Dam floating solar power project by Korea Water Resources Corporation [141]. Gurye County is promoting a plan to generate basic income for residents from profits obtained through renewable energy generation, simultaneously pursuing local economic revitalization and energy transition [116, 118, 119, 122, 132, 133].

sections.title: 🌍 International Climate Response and Challenges
sections.content: The international community is also accelerating its response to climate change but faces various challenges. The European Union (EU) has set a target to reduce greenhouse gas emissions by 90% by 2040 compared to 1990, expressing strong determination [15]. While the UK showed a positive change with a 2% reduction in greenhouse gas emissions [136], criticisms persist that China's coal use and the U.S.'s carbon dioxide emissions are exacerbating global warming [146]. Climate change is threatening human health in South Africa [147], negatively impacting coffee and mango production [149, 157, 159], and causing severe damage globally, including increasing the frequency of large-scale wildfires [155]. Meanwhile, active community participation in combating climate change continues, with students in Newark and East Bay creating 'pocket forests' on campus [152, 153, 154]. The issue of declining consumer trust due to suspicions of greenwashing regarding companies' eco-friendly claims is also a matter that cannot be overlooked [14].

briefing.closing: As we have reviewed in today's briefing, the threat of climate change is becoming increasingly clear. However, at the same time, domestic and international efforts and innovations for carbon neutrality and renewable energy transition are continuously expanding. Sustained interest and participation are crucial to ensure these efforts lead to tangible results. This concludes today's climate briefing.

summary.cause: This is due to a combination of cocoa shortages, climate shocks, and global conflicts, which have collectively impacted the chocolate economy.

summary.phenomenon: Google has planned a partnership with a natural gas power plant to supply electricity to its data center in Texas. This plant is projected to emit 4.5 million tons of carbon dioxide annually, exceeding the total emissions of the entire city of San Francisco.
summary.cause: This move is intended to meet the power demands of Google's AI data centers, marking a significant shift from Google's previous climate goal of achieving carbon neutrality by 2030.
summary.outlook: If this plan is implemented, the annual emission of 4.5 million tons of carbon dioxide is expected to negatively impact Google's progress toward its carbon neutrality goals.
